subject: Maths
text: Count reliably with numbers from 1 to 20, place them in order and say which number is one more or one less than a given number.

subject: Maths
text: Use everyday language to talk about size, weight, capacity, position, distance, time and money to compare quantities and objects.

subject: Maths
text: Using quantities and objects, add and subtract two single-digit numbers and count on or back to find the answer.

subject: English
text: Link sounds to letters, naming and sounding the letters of the alphabet, and begin to read words and simple sentences.

subject: English
text: Use a pencil and hold it effectively to form recognisable letters and begin to write own name and words.

subject: English
text: Listen attentively in a range of situations, follow instructions, and develop vocabulary by being exposed to new words in context.

subject: Science
text: Explore and observe the natural world including animals and plants; begin to make sense of the physical world through exploration.

subject: Science
text: Talk about features of their immediate environment and how environments vary across the seasons and in different places.

subject: Maths
text: Count to and across 100, forwards and backwards; read and write numbers from 1 to 100 in numerals and words; identify one more and one less.

subject: Maths
text: Represent and use number bonds and related subtraction facts within 20; add and subtract one-digit and two-digit numbers to 20.

subject: Maths
text: Solve one-step problems involving multiplication and division using concrete objects, pictorial representations and arrays.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise, find and name a half as one of two equal parts and a quarter as one of four equal parts of a shape or quantity.

subject: Maths
text: Compare, describe and solve practical problems for lengths, weights/masses, capacity/volume and time; measure and record.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise and name common 2-D and 3-D shapes including rectangles, squares, circles, triangles, cuboids, pyramids and spheres.

subject: Maths
text: Describe position, direction and movement including whole, half, quarter and three-quarter turns.

subject: English
text: Apply phonic knowledge and skills as the primary route to decode words; read aloud phonically decodable books accurately.

subject: English
text: Develop pleasure in reading and vocabulary by listening to and discussing a wide range of poems, stories and non-fiction.

subject: English
text: Sequence sentences to form short narratives; use capital letters, full stops, question marks and exclamation marks correctly.

subject: English
text: Sit correctly at a table and form lower-case letters in the correct direction, starting and finishing in the right place.

subject: English
text: Spell words containing each of the 40+ phonemes and common exception words for Year 1; name the letters of the alphabet in order.

subject: Science
text: Identify and name a variety of common wild and garden plants including deciduous and evergreen trees; describe the basic structure of flowering plants.

subject: Science
text: Identify and name a variety of common animals; describe and compare the structure of animals including fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals.

subject: Science
text: Identify, name, draw and label the basic parts of the human body and say which part of the body is associated with each sense.

subject: Science
text: Distinguish between an object and the material from which it is made; identify and name a variety of everyday materials.

subject: Science
text: Observe changes across the four seasons; observe and describe weather associated with the seasons and how day length varies.

subject: History
text: Develop awareness of the past using common words and phrases relating to time; discuss changes within and beyond living memory.

subject: History
text: Learn about the lives of significant individuals in the past who have contributed to national and international achievements.

subject: Geography
text: Observe and describe seasonal and daily weather patterns in the United Kingdom and how they affect daily life.

subject: Geography
text: Name and locate the world's seven continents and five oceans; name and locate the four countries of the UK and their capital cities.

subject: Geography
text: Understand geographical similarities and differences through studying the human and physical geography of their local area.

subject: Computing
text: Understand what algorithms are and how they are implemented as programs; create and debug simple programs using logical reasoning.

subject: Art and Design
text: Use a range of materials creatively to design and make products; use drawing, painting and sculpture to develop and share ideas.

subject: Music
text: Use voice expressively by singing songs and speaking chants and rhymes; listen with concentration to music from different traditions.

subject: PE
text: Master basic movements including running, jumping, throwing and catching; develop balance, agility and co-ordination.

subject: RE
text: Explore different religious beliefs, stories and practices; begin to understand the importance of religion for believers.

subject: PSHE
text: Recognise and manage feelings; understand how to keep healthy and safe; develop positive relationships with others.

subject: Design and Technology
text: Design purposeful, functional and appealing products; select from and use a range of tools and materials to perform practical tasks.

subject: French
text: Listen and respond to simple spoken French; begin to recognise and use basic vocabulary for greetings, numbers and colours.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise the place value of each digit in a two-digit number; compare and order numbers from 0 up to 100; use <, > and = signs.

subject: Maths
text: Add and subtract numbers using concrete objects, pictorial representations and mental methods including two-digit numbers.

subject: Maths
text: Recall and use multiplication and division facts for the 2, 5 and 10 multiplication tables; write multiplication and division statements.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise, find, name and write fractions 1/3, 1/4, 2/4 and 3/4 of a length, shape, set of objects or quantity.

subject: Maths
text: Choose and use appropriate standard units to estimate and measure length/height, mass, temperature and capacity; tell and write the time to five minutes.

subject: Maths
text: Identify and describe properties of 2-D and 3-D shapes including the number of sides, vertices, edges and faces; identify lines of symmetry.

subject: Maths
text: Interpret and construct simple pictograms, tally charts, block diagrams and tables; ask and answer simple questions about totalling and comparing.

subject: English
text: Write for different purposes using expanded noun phrases; develop positive attitudes towards writing and stamina for writing at length.

subject: English
text: Learn how to use punctuation correctly including apostrophes for contraction, commas in lists and inverted commas for direct speech.

subject: English
text: Read aloud books; develop understanding by predicting, inferring and asking questions; check that the text makes sense.

subject: English
text: Segment spoken words into phonemes; add suffixes to spell longer words; learn words from the Year 2 statutory word list.

subject: Science
text: Identify and compare the suitability of everyday materials for particular uses; find out how the shapes of solid objects can be changed.

subject: Science
text: Explore habitats and the animals that live in them; identify food chains and the conditions that animals need to survive.

subject: Science
text: Observe and describe how seeds and bulbs grow into mature plants; find out what plants need to grow and stay healthy.

subject: Science
text: Find out about and describe the basic needs of animals for survival; describe the importance of exercise, eating the right amount of food and hygiene.

subject: History
text: Develop awareness of the past through events beyond living memory that are significant nationally and place them on a simple timeline.

subject: History
text: Learn about the lives of significant individuals in the past who have contributed to national and international achievements.

subject: Geography
text: Name and locate the world's seven continents and five oceans; locate the countries and capital cities of the United Kingdom.

subject: Geography
text: Identify seasonal and daily weather patterns in the UK and locate hot and cold areas of the world in relation to the Equator and the Poles.

subject: Computing
text: Design, write and debug programs that accomplish specific goals; use sequence in programs and detect and correct errors.

subject: Art and Design
text: Use drawing, painting and sculpture to develop and share ideas; learn about the work of a range of artists and designers.

subject: Music
text: Play tuned and untuned instruments musically; listen with concentration; begin to experiment with creating short compositions.

subject: PE
text: Participate in team games, developing simple tactics; perform dances using simple movement patterns; develop coordination and balance.

subject: PSHE
text: Understand the importance of physical activity; know how to stay safe and when to ask for help; recognise and manage feelings.

subject: Design and Technology
text: Explore and use mechanisms such as levers, sliders, wheels and axles in products; design, make and evaluate functional products.

subject: RE
text: Learn about religious and cultural celebrations; understand how they are observed and make connections between stories and beliefs.

subject: French
text: Listen attentively and respond to spoken French; use basic vocabulary for greetings, numbers, colours and classroom objects.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise the place value of each digit in a three-digit number; count from 0 in multiples of 4, 8, 50 and 100; round to nearest 10 or 100.

subject: Maths
text: Add and subtract numbers with up to three digits using formal written methods of columnar addition and subtraction; estimate the answer to a calculation.

subject: Maths
text: Recall and use multiplication and division facts for the 3, 4 and 8 multiplication tables; write and calculate mathematical statements for multiplication and division.

subject: Maths
text: Count up and down in tenths; recognise and find fractions of objects and quantities; recognise and show equivalent fractions.

subject: Maths
text: Measure, compare, add and subtract lengths, mass/weight, volume and capacity; measure the perimeter of simple 2-D shapes; tell and write the time from an analogue clock.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise angles as a property of shape; identify right angles, perpendicular and parallel lines; describe properties of 2-D and 3-D shapes.

subject: Maths
text: Interpret and present data using bar charts, pictograms and tables; solve one and two-step questions using information in scaled bar charts.

subject: English
text: Organise writing into paragraphs around a theme; use headings and sub-headings to aid presentation; write non-fiction texts.

subject: English
text: Extend sentences using a wider range of conjunctions; use conjunctions, adverbs and prepositions to express time, place and cause.

subject: English
text: Listen to and discuss a broad range of fiction, poetry, plays and non-fiction; predict and infer meaning from texts.

subject: English
text: Use further prefixes and suffixes to spell longer words; use dictionaries to check spelling; learn Year 3 statutory word list.

subject: Science
text: Compare how things move on different surfaces; observe that some forces need contact between two objects; describe how magnets attract and repel.

subject: Science
text: Recognise that they need light to see things; notice that light is reflected from surfaces; describe how shadows are formed and find patterns in how they change.

subject: Science
text: Compare and group rocks based on appearance and physical properties; describe how fossils are formed; recognise that soils are made from rocks and organic matter.

subject: Science
text: Identify that animals including humans need the right types and amount of nutrition; identify that humans and some other animals have skeletons and muscles for support, protection and movement.

subject: Science
text: Identify and describe the functions of different parts of flowering plants; explore the requirements of plants for life and growth; investigate seed dispersal.

subject: History
text: Construct informed responses about changes in Britain from the Stone Age to the Iron Age including hunter-gatherers and early farmers.

subject: History
text: Study Ancient Egypt as a civilisation, exploring beliefs, achievements and social structure including the role of pharaohs and the importance of the River Nile.

subject: Geography
text: Describe key aspects of human geography including types of settlement, land use, economic activity and trade links.

subject: Geography
text: Describe key aspects of physical geography including rivers, mountains, volcanoes and earthquakes; understand their impact on people.

subject: Geography
text: Locate the countries of Europe using maps; name and locate major cities; understand geographical similarities and differences.

subject: Computing
text: Understand computer networks including the internet; appreciate how information is shared across networks; understand online safety.

subject: Computing
text: Design and write programs using sequence and repetition; use variables; use logical reasoning to debug programs.

subject: Art and Design
text: Improve mastery of art and design techniques including drawing, painting and sculpture; learn about great artists and their work.

subject: Music
text: Use musical vocabulary; begin to use staff notation; compose music using structure and a range of musical elements.

subject: PE
text: Use running, jumping, throwing and catching in isolation and in combination; apply skills in games; develop flexibility and strength.

subject: PSHE
text: Understand how to maintain healthy relationships; recognise bullying and how to respond; learn about online safety.

subject: Design and Technology
text: Understand and use structural components; design, make and evaluate structures that are stable and load-bearing.

subject: RE
text: Learn about the practices and beliefs of different religious traditions; explore sacred texts and places of worship.

subject: French
text: Listen attentively and respond to spoken French; begin to read simple French words and phrases; use numbers, colours and everyday vocabulary.

subject: Maths
text: Count in multiples of 6, 7, 9, 25 and 1000; recognise the place value of each digit in a four-digit number; round any number to the nearest 10, 100 or 1000.

subject: Maths
text: Add and subtract numbers with up to 4 digits using efficient written methods; estimate and use inverse operations to check answers.

subject: Maths
text: Recall multiplication and division facts for multiplication tables up to 12 × 12; use place value and mental methods to multiply and divide.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise and show families of common equivalent fractions; add and subtract fractions with the same denominator; count up and down in hundredths.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise and write decimal equivalents of any number of tenths or hundredths; compare numbers with the same number of decimal places up to two decimal places.

subject: Maths
text: Convert between different units of measure; measure and calculate the perimeter of a rectilinear figure; find the area of rectilinear shapes by counting squares.

subject: Maths
text: Compare and classify geometric shapes based on their properties; identify acute and obtuse angles; identify lines of symmetry; describe positions on a 2-D grid.

subject: Maths
text: Interpret and present discrete and continuous data using appropriate graphical methods; solve comparison, sum and difference problems.

subject: English
text: Use fronted adverbials and commas after fronted adverbials; use and punctuate direct speech; use Standard English and understand informal speech.

subject: English
text: Identify and discuss themes and conventions across a wide range of writing; justify inferences with evidence from the text.

subject: English
text: Plan, draft and write narratives creating settings, characters and plot; evaluate and edit writing by checking for errors in grammar, punctuation and spelling.

subject: English
text: Use further prefixes and suffixes; use word families based on common words; spell words from the Year 4 statutory word list.

subject: Science
text: Construct a simple series electrical circuit; identify and name the components; predict whether a lamp will light; recognise conductors and insulators.

subject: Science
text: Describe the simple functions of the basic parts of the digestive system; identify different types of teeth; construct and interpret varied food chains.

subject: Science
text: Identify how sounds are made when objects vibrate; find patterns between pitch of sound and features of the object; understand that sounds get fainter with distance.

subject: Science
text: Compare and group materials according to whether they are solids, liquids or gases; observe materials change state when heated or cooled; describe the water cycle.

subject: History
text: Understand the impact of Roman settlement and legacy in Britain including invasion, resistance, infrastructure and everyday life.

subject: History
text: Study ancient Greece as a civilisation and its influence on the western world including democracy, art, architecture and the Olympic Games.

subject: Geography
text: Describe and understand the physical geography of rivers including erosion, deposition and the water cycle; name and locate major rivers.

subject: Geography
text: Understand the geographical features of tropical rainforests; explore the impact of deforestation and environmental challenges.

subject: Geography
text: Locate Asia's countries using maps; identify key physical features such as mountain ranges and rivers; compare with the UK.

subject: Computing
text: Use sequence, selection and repetition in programs; work with variables; design and create programs that accomplish specific goals.

subject: Computing
text: Select, use and combine software to collect, organise and present data; analyse data to answer questions.

subject: Art and Design
text: Develop art and design techniques; explore the work of artists, craftspeople and designers from different cultures and time periods.

subject: Music
text: Play and perform in ensemble contexts; improvise and compose using musical elements; read and write musical notation.

subject: PE
text: Apply a broader range of skills; learn how to use skills in different ways; work effectively in a team using attacking and defending tactics.

subject: PSHE
text: Understand physical and emotional changes; develop strategies for managing feelings; understand healthy relationships and online safety.

subject: Design and Technology
text: Apply understanding of computing to program and control products; design and make products with electrical or mechanical systems.

subject: RE
text: Explore the beliefs, practices and key figures of major world religions; make connections between beliefs and ways of life.

subject: French
text: Engage in conversations about familiar topics; read and write simple sentences in French; understand grammatical patterns including gender of nouns.

subject: Maths
text: Read, write, order and compare numbers to at least 1,000,000 and determine the value of each digit; round any number up to 1,000,000 to the nearest 10, 100, 1,000, 10,000 and 100,000.

subject: Maths
text: Add and subtract whole numbers with more than 4 digits using formal written methods; use rounding to check answers; solve multi-step problems.

subject: Maths
text: Multiply numbers up to 4 digits by a two-digit number using long multiplication; identify prime numbers, factors, multiples; recognise and use square and cube numbers.

subject: Maths
text: Convert between mixed numbers and improper fractions; compare and order fractions with different denominators; add and subtract fractions with the same and different denominators.

subject: Maths
text: Recognise and use the percent symbol; understand percentages as fractions with denominator 100; write percentages as a fraction and as a decimal.

subject: Maths
text: Calculate and compare the area of rectangles; estimate the volume and capacity of cuboids; convert between different units of metric measure.

subject: Maths
text: Identify 3-D shapes from 2-D representations; know angles are measured in degrees; estimate, draw and measure angles; use coordinates in all four quadrants.

subject: Maths
text: Complete, read and interpret information in tables including timetables; calculate and interpret the mean as an average.

subject: Maths
text: Solve problems involving ratio and proportion; understand relative sizes and scales; solve problems involving scaling by simple fractions.

subject: English
text: Use relative clauses beginning with who, which, where, when, whose, that; use modal verbs to indicate degrees of possibility; use colons and semicolons to mark boundaries.

subject: English
text: Plan, draft and write narratives with complex characters and settings; integrate dialogue to convey character; create atmosphere and tension.

subject: English
text: Write for a range of real purposes and audiences; distinguish between formal and informal registers; write persuasively with reasoned argument.

subject: English
text: Draw inferences and justify with evidence; identify how language, structure and presentation contribute to meaning; discuss and evaluate how authors use language.

subject: Science
text: Describe the movement of Earth and other planets relative to the Sun; describe the movement of the Moon relative to Earth; explain day and night using the rotation of the Earth.

subject: Science
text: Explain that unsupported objects fall towards Earth because of gravity; identify effects of air and water resistance and friction; recognise that simple machines allow a smaller force to have a greater effect.

subject: Science
text: Describe the life cycles of animals including mammals, amphibians, insects and birds; describe the changes as humans develop to old age.

subject: Science
text: Compare and group materials based on their properties; explain that some materials dissolve to form a solution; use knowledge to decide how to recover substances from mixtures.

subject: History
text: Develop detailed understanding of Ancient Egyptian civilisation including beliefs, achievements, social structure and the role of the Nile.

subject: History
text: Study Britain's settlement by Anglo-Saxons and Vikings; understand the struggle for the Kingdom of England to the time of Edward the Confessor.

subject: History
text: Study the causes and key events of the Second World War; understand the impact on Britain and the wider world including evacuation, the Blitz and the Holocaust.

subject: Geography
text: Understand geographical similarities and differences through the study of biomes and climate zones; describe key aspects of physical geography.

subject: Geography
text: Locate the countries of North and South America; describe key physical and human characteristics; compare with UK geography.

subject: Geography
text: Understand the distribution of natural resources and economic activity; explore concepts of fair trade and globalisation.

subject: Computing
text: Use a text-based programming language; understand how to design, write and debug programs using functions and variables.

subject: Computing
text: Understand how computer networks including the internet work; evaluate online information for reliability; understand copyright and data security.

subject: Art and Design
text: Develop mastery of art and design techniques; study great artists from different cultures; explore perspective, portraiture and mixed media.

subject: Music
text: Play and perform with increasing confidence; improvise within given structures; compose using more complex musical forms; read musical notation.

subject: PE
text: Apply and develop a broader range of skills; evaluate and recognise their own success; compare performances with previous ones.

subject: PSHE
text: Understand mental health and wellbeing strategies; explore puberty and healthy relationships; develop financial literacy and career awareness.

subject: Design and Technology
text: Understand and apply the principles of a healthy and varied diet; cook a range of savoury dishes; understand seasonality and provenance of ingredients.

subject: RE
text: Explore ethical and moral questions from a religious perspective; learn about pilgrimage, rites of passage and religious codes of conduct.

subject: French
text: Use French past and future tenses; express and justify opinions; read and write extended passages; develop cultural understanding of Francophone countries.

subject: Maths
text: Read, write, order and compare numbers to at least 10,000,000; round any whole number to a required degree of accuracy; use negative numbers in context.

subject: Maths
text: Perform mental calculations with mixed operations and large numbers; use order of operations; solve multi-step problems involving fractions, decimals and percentages.

subject: Maths
text: Multiply multi-digit numbers up to 4 digits by a two-digit whole number using long multiplication; divide numbers up to 4 digits by a two-digit number using long division.

subject: Maths
text: Use common factors to simplify fractions; add and subtract fractions with different denominators; multiply simple pairs of proper fractions; divide proper fractions by whole numbers.

subject: Maths
text: Solve problems involving the relative sizes of two quantities where missing values can be found by using integer multiplication and division; use ratio to solve scaling problems.

subject: Maths
text: Use simple formulae; generate and describe linear number sequences; express missing number problems algebraically; find pairs of numbers that satisfy an equation.

subject: Maths
text: Solve problems involving calculation and conversion of units; calculate area of parallelograms and triangles; calculate, estimate and compare volume of cubes and cuboids.

subject: Maths
text: Draw 2-D shapes using given dimensions and angles; recognise, describe and build 3-D shapes; find unknown angles in triangles, quadrilaterals and regular polygons.

subject: Maths
text: Interpret and construct pie charts and line graphs; calculate and interpret the mean as an average; solve comparison, sum and difference problems.

subject: Maths
text: Apply mathematical reasoning to solve increasingly complex problems; explain methods and justify reasoning; prepare for KS2 assessments.

subject: English
text: Use the passive voice; use the subjunctive form; use a range of cohesive devices; distinguish between formal and informal registers.

subject: English
text: Plan, draft and write narratives with complex characters, settings and plot structures; use literary devices including metaphor, simile and personification.

subject: English
text: Read and discuss an increasing range of fiction, poetry, plays, non-fiction; evaluate how authors use language and structure to achieve effects.

subject: English
text: Consolidate grammar, punctuation and spelling knowledge in preparation for KS2 assessments; apply understanding across different text types.

subject: Science
text: Recognise that living things have changed over time; that fossils provide information about the past; identify how animals and plants are adapted to their environment.

subject: Science
text: Describe how living things are classified into broad groups; give reasons for classifying plants and animals based on specific characteristics; use classification keys.

subject: Science
text: Associate the brightness of a lamp or volume of a buzzer with the number and voltage of cells; use recognised symbols when representing a circuit diagram.

subject: Science
text: Recognise that light travels in straight lines; explain that we see objects because they give out or reflect light into the eye; explain shadows and refraction.

subject: Science
text: Identify the main parts of the human circulatory system; describe the functions of the heart, blood vessels and blood; recognise the impact of diet, exercise and lifestyle.

subject: History
text: Study the causes and key events of World War One; understand life in the trenches and the war's impact on Britain and the British Empire.

subject: History
text: Study a significant turning point in world history such as the civil rights movement; evaluate its long-term impact on society.

subject: History
text: Understand the development of democracy, parliament and the monarchy in Britain; explore key historical turning points including Magna Carta.

subject: Geography
text: Understand human geography including economic activity, trade links and the distribution of natural resources including energy, food, minerals and water.

subject: Geography
text: Locate Africa's countries; study physical and human characteristics; compare quality of life and development indicators between African and UK regions.

subject: Geography
text: Understand the causes and consequences of climate change; explore sustainability and how individuals and nations can respond to environmental challenges.

subject: Computing
text: Design, write and debug programs; use procedures and functions in a text-based language; understand data types and apply computational thinking.

subject: Computing
text: Understand cybersecurity principles; protect digital privacy; evaluate online information critically; understand the risks of sharing personal data.

subject: Art and Design
text: Develop art and design skills to a high level; study great artists and designers; create work inspired by their style; evaluate their own and others' work critically.

subject: Music
text: Perform with increasing confidence, skill and expressiveness; compose music to create a specific effect; analyse music from different historical periods and genres.

subject: PE
text: Develop leadership and coaching skills; take part in competitive sport; evaluate performance and lead others in physical activity.

subject: PSHE
text: Prepare for transition to secondary school; understand mental health strategies; explore identity and self-esteem; develop understanding of healthy relationships and consent.

subject: Design and Technology
text: Understand and use mechanical systems including gears and cams; apply design principles to create sophisticated products that solve real problems.

subject: RE
text: Explore philosophical and ethical questions; compare religious and non-religious worldviews; evaluate different responses to moral dilemmas.

subject: French
text: Use a range of tenses and grammatical structures; engage in extended conversations; read and respond to longer texts; develop cultural understanding of Francophone countries.
